target.title: fix(agents): gate owned-write publish on pre-append fingerprint (#86572)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` in vanilla openclaw caused by pi's stream iteration writing via direct `appendFileSync` between `releaseForPrompt` and the next external-hook lock acquisition. The lane's own writes are recorded in the existing `ownedSessionFileWrites` machinery via `publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(beforeWrite)`, gated on the **pre-append** fingerprint matching the trusted fence. External mutations — including those interleaved before pi's own append — never pass the gate and still trip the takeover fence.

Closes #86572.

## Revision history

- `91ba92c9ab` — original `refreshBeforeLock` shim. ClawSweeper flagged blanket-refresh weakened takeover detection.
- `d824be0298` — `publishOwnedPostMessageWrite()` gated on `fenceFingerprint`. ClawSweeper flagged the mixed external-then-pi interleave: when an external write landed between `releaseForPrompt` and pi's append, `fenceFingerprint` was still trusted, so the combined post-write state (external + pi) got recorded as owned.
- **`584ba0e65b`** (HEAD) — gate switched to `beforeWrite` (pre-append fingerprint passed in by the caller). External mutations between trust point and pi's append fail the gate; mixed-interleave negative test added.

## Root cause

`_handleAgentEvent` → `sessionManager.appendMessage` → `_persist` uses synchronous `appendFileSync`. The session-tool-result-guard fires `onMessagePersisted` immediately after that synchronous append. The previous fence-refresh in `onMessagePersisted` only updated the local fence, never recording the write as owned — so a hook firing later in the same prompt window saw `current` ≠ `fenceFingerprint` and tripped.

## Fix

### 1. New `publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(beforeWrite)` method

`EmbeddedAttemptSessionLockController` gains:

```ts
publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(beforeWrite: SessionFileFingerprint | undefined): void {
  if (takeoverDetected) return;
  if (!beforeWrite) return;
  // Trust gate: beforeWrite is the file fingerprint captured immediately
  // BEFORE pi's appendFileSync. If something external landed between
  // releaseForPrompt (which trusted F0) and this append, beforeWrite = F1
  // ≠ F0 and isTrustedSessionFileState returns false — fail closed.
  if (!isTrustedSessionFileState(sessionFileFenceKey, beforeWrite)) return;
  const current = readSessionFileFingerprintSync(params.lockOptions.sessionFile);
  if (sameSessionFileFingerprint(beforeWrite, current)) return;
  const generation = recordOwnedSessionFileWrite(sessionFileFenceKey, current);
  if (fenceActive) {
    fenceFingerprint = current;
    fenceSnapshot = { fingerprint: current };
    fenceGeneration = generation;
  }
}
```

### 2. Plumb the pre-append fingerprint through `session-tool-result-guard`

The guard gains an optional `beforeMessagePersist` hook that runs immediately before `originalAppend(message)`. Its return value flows back through `onMessagePersisted`'s new context arg:

```ts
beforeMessagePersist?: () => unknown;
onMessagePersisted?: (
  message: AgentMessage,
  context: { beforeWriteSnapshot?: unknown },
) => void | Promise<void>;
```

Type stays `unknown` so `session-tool-result-guard` doesn't depend on `SessionFileFingerprint`'s structure.

### 3. Wire at the call site (`runEmbeddedAttempt`)

```ts
beforeMessagePersist: () => readSessionFileFingerprintSync(params.sessionFile),
onMessagePersisted: (_message, { beforeWriteSnapshot }) => {
  sessionLockController.publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(
    beforeWriteSnapshot as SessionFileFingerprint | undefined,
  );
},
```

### Why this addresses the bot's concern

ClawSweeper required:

> *"Capture the session-file fingerprint immediately before the pi/session-manager append that triggers onMessagePersisted, pass that baseline into the session-lock controller, record the post-write fingerprint only when the baseline matches the trusted fence, and add a regression where an external append happens between releaseForPrompt and the pi append and still trips the hook lock."*

All four parts are in this PR:
- **Capture pre-append baseline:** `beforeMessagePersist` runs *before* `originalAppend(message)`.
- **Pass into controller:** flows through `onMessagePersisted`'s context arg.
- **Record only when baseline matches trusted fence:** `isTrustedSessionFileState(key, beforeWrite)` is the gate.
- **Mixed-interleave regression test:** new test `trips takeover on a mixed external-then-pi append (publish refuses to launder external mutation)`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Eliminates spurious `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` on the lane's own pi-driven writes when an external hook fires after rapid pi writes. The takeover fence still trips on genuine external mutations AND on mixed external-then-pi interleaves.

- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout (macOS 15.5, Node 22.19+) at the patched branch HEAD `584ba0e65b`, branched off `3eb06e305e` (current upstream main). The new regression tests exercise the production `installSessionExternalHookWriteLock` + `EmbeddedAttemptSessionLockController` + `installSessionPersistenceGuard` machinery against real temp session JSONL files via `fs.appendFile`. The proof harness loads the controller from source and drives four scenarios end-to-end against a real temp session file.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  ```sh
  pnpm install
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.transcript-events.test.ts src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.tool-result-persist-hook.test.ts
  npx tsx /Users/umank/code/openclaw-tickets/proof/run-refresh-before-hook-lock-proof.mjs
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:**

  **(a) Four-scenario proof harness** — verbatim stdout:

  ```
  ============================================================
  PR #86584 — publish-on-onMessagePersisted proof (Issue #86572)
  ============================================================
  source under test: src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts
                     publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(beforeWrite)
                     gated on isTrustedSessionFileState(key, beforeWrite)

  -------- Scenario A — pre-fix: pi-style write WITHOUT publish (trips takeover) --------
    setup       mode=pre-fix
    lifecycle   releaseForPrompt() — capture fence F0 + mark trusted
    pi-write    pi appendFileSync (F0 → F1) — no publish call
    hook        beforeToolCall() — wrapped by installSessionExternalHookWriteLock
    hook        RESULT: TAKEOVER (EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError)
    state       controller.hasSessionTakeover() = true

  -------- Scenario B — post-fix: pi-style write WITH publish (hook fires cleanly) --------
    setup       mode=post-fix
    lifecycle   releaseForPrompt() — capture fence F0 + mark trusted
    pi-write    pi appendFileSync (F0 → F1) with pre-write captured
    publish     publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(F0) — F0 is trusted, record F1 as owned
    hook        beforeToolCall()
    hook        RESULT: hook fired successfully (hookOutcome.fired=true)
    state       controller.hasSessionTakeover() = false

  -------- Scenario C — negative: external write bypasses publish (still trips) --------
    setup       mode=external-only
    lifecycle   releaseForPrompt() — capture fence F0 + mark trusted
    pi-write    EXTERNAL appendFileSync (F0 → F1) — no publish
    hook        beforeToolCall()
    hook        RESULT: TAKEOVER (EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError)
    state       controller.hasSessionTakeover() = true

  -------- Scenario D — mixed interleave: external THEN pi write + publish (still trips) --------
    setup       mode=mixed-interleave
    lifecycle   releaseForPrompt() — capture fence F0 + mark trusted
    external    EXTERNAL appendFileSync first (F0 → F1)
    pi-write    pi appendFileSync second (F1 → F2) — pre-write captured = F1
    publish     publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(F1) — F1 NOT trusted, skip (fail closed)
    hook        beforeToolCall()
    hook        RESULT: TAKEOVER (EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError)
    state       controller.hasSessionTakeover() = true

  ============================================================
    Scenario A (no fix):       hook fired=false, threw=TAKEOVER
    Scenario B (with fix):     hook fired=true, threw=no
    Scenario C (negative):     hook fired=false, threw=TAKEOVER
    Scenario D (mixed):        hook fired=false, threw=TAKEOVER

    RESULT: PASS
  ============================================================
  ```

  Scenario D is the **bot-requested mixed-interleaving regression**. Without the pre-append gate, the publish would record F2 as owned (because F0 is trusted at publish time) and the external mutation would be laundered. With the gate, F1 (pre-append after external) is checked — it's not trusted — publish skips — fence trips correctly on the combined state.

  **(b) Test suite** — 80 cases across 2 test files green:

  ```
   Test Files  2 passed (2)
        Tests  80 passed (80)
  ```

  Key new/updated tests under "embedded attempt session lock lifecycle":

  - `accepts pi-style writes published via publishOwnedPostMessageWrite before beforeToolCall fires` — positive case (passes pre-write F0).
  - `trips takeover on a same-file external write that bypasses publishOwnedPostMessageWrite` — basic negative case.
  - **`trips takeover on a mixed external-then-pi append (publish refuses to launder external mutation)`** — the bot-required mixed-interleave regression.
  - `allows multiple prompt turns with pi-style writes published per turn` — multi-turn, three iterations.
  - `does not classify a different session file's writes as owned by this controller` — cross-file isolation.
  - `does not hang when reacquireAfterPrompt rejects after a pi-style direct write` — abort/error path.

  Adjacent session-tool-result-guard test surfaces (4 files, 46 cases) also green; no regressions from the new `beforeMessagePersist` opt.

- **Observed result after fix:** All four harness scenarios PASS as expected, including the mixed-interleave fail-closed check. `pnpm check:changed` clean (typecheck/lint/cycles/etc.).

- **What was not tested:** A live gateway-fallback reproduction against the patched build on real workload. The local repro that @cby2k has (session `4f181fe9-…`) is the most direct validation target; the patched build is being prepared on the user's GB10 node for that confirmation. The test suite plus the four-scenario proof harness cover the structural invariants — including the mixed-interleave case the bot required.

## Compatibility / Risk

- `publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(beforeWrite)` is a new method; only one call site (`onMessagePersisted` in `runEmbeddedAttempt`). Not part of any external API.
- `session-tool-result-guard`'s `onMessagePersisted` signature gains an optional `context` arg. The only internal caller is `runEmbeddedAttempt`; no external consumers found via grep.
- `session-tool-result-guard` gains an optional `beforeMessagePersist` hook. Backward-compatible (no caller required to provide it). When omitted, behavior is unchanged.
- **Does not weaken takeover detection** — the pre-append trust gate ensures only writes preceded by a trusted-fence baseline get recorded as owned. External mutations that bypass the callback, OR external mutations that interleave with pi's own append, both fail the gate.
- `refreshAfterOwnedSessionWrite` retained on the controller for existing call sites in tests; production wiring no longer uses it from `onMessagePersisted`.

## Related

- Issue #86572 — root cause analysis and acceptance criteria.
- #85913 — original `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` tracking issue; cby2k's deterministic repro.
- #86067 — file-scoped prompt-window guard (cross-lane race) — independent fix; this PR addresses the same-lane race.
- ClawSweeper reviews on `91ba92c9ab` and `d824be0298` drove the design through two iterations; the trust gate's parameter shape (pre-append vs current) was the key correctness pivot.

---

*This PR is AI-assisted. Code authored with Claude.*

target.body:

  pnpm install

  npx tsx /Users/umank/code/openclaw-tickets/proof/run-refresh-before-hook-lock-proof.mjs
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:**

  **(a) Four-scenario proof harness** — verbatim stdout:

  ```
  ============================================================
  PR #86584 — publish-on-onMessagePersisted proof (Issue #86572)
  ============================================================
  source under test: src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts
                     publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(beforeWrite)
                     gated on isTrustedSessionFileState(key, beforeWrite)

  -------- Scenario A — pre-fix: pi-style write WITHOUT publish (trips takeover) --------

    lifecycle   releaseForPrompt() — capture fence F0 + mark trusted
    pi-write    pi appendFileSync (F0 → F1) — no publish call

  -------- Scenario B — post-fix: pi-style write WITH publish (hook fires cleanly) --------

    hook        beforeToolCall()
    hook        RESULT: hook fired successfully (hookOutcome.fired=true)

    lifecycle   releaseForPrompt() — capture fence F0 + mark trusted
    pi-write    EXTERNAL appendFileSync (F0 → F1) — no publish
    hook        beforeToolCall()
    hook        RESULT: TAKEOVER (EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError)
    state       controller.hasSessionTakeover() = true

  -------- Scenario D — mixed interleave: external THEN pi write + publish (still trips) --------
    setup       mode=mixed-interleave
    lifecycle   releaseForPrompt() — capture fence F0 + mark trusted
    external    EXTERNAL appendFileSync first (F0 → F1)
    pi-write    pi appendFileSync second (F1 → F2) — pre-write captured = F1
    publish     publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(F1) — F1 NOT trusted, skip (fail closed)
    hook        beforeToolCall()
    hook        RESULT: TAKEOVER (EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError)
    state       controller.hasSessionTakeover() = true

  ============================================================
    Scenario A (no fix):       hook fired=false, threw=TAKEOVER
    Scenario B (with fix):     hook fired=true, threw=no
    Scenario C (negative):     hook fired=false, threw=TAKEOVER
    Scenario D (mixed):        hook fired=false, threw=TAKEOVER

    RESULT: PASS
  ============================================================
  ```

  Scenario D is the **bot-requested mixed-interleaving regression**. Without the pre-append gate, the publish would record F2 as owned (because F0 is trusted at publish time) and the external mutation would be laundered. With the gate, F1 (pre-append after external) is checked — it's not trusted — publish skips — fence trips correctly on the combined state.

  **(b) Test suite** — 80 cases across 2 test files green:

  ```
   Test Files  2 passed (2)
        Tests  80 passed (80)
  ```

  - `accepts pi-style writes published via publishOwnedPostMessageWrite before beforeToolCall fires` — positive case (passes pre-write F0).
  - `trips takeover on a same-file external write that bypasses publishOwnedPostMessageWrite` — basic negative case.

  - `allows multiple prompt turns with pi-style writes published per turn` — multi-turn, three iterations.
  - `does not classify a different session file's writes as owned by this controller` — cross-file isolation.
  - `does not hang when reacquireAfterPrompt rejects after a pi-style direct write` — abort/error path.

- **What was not tested:** A live gateway-fallback reproduction against the patched build on real workload. - #85913 — original `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` tracking issue; cby2k's deterministic repro.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Eliminates spurious `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` on the lane's own pi-driven writes when an external hook fires after rapid pi writes. The takeover fence still trips on genuine external mutations AND on mixed external-then-pi interleaves.

- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout (macOS 15.5, Node 22.19+) at the patched branch HEAD `584ba0e65b`, branched off `3eb06e305e` (current upstream main). The new regression tests exercise the production `installSessionExternalHookWriteLock` + `EmbeddedAttemptSessionLockController` + `installSessionPersistenceGuard` machinery against real temp session JSONL files via `fs.appendFile`. The proof harness loads the controller from source and drives four scenarios end-to-end against a real temp session file.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  ```sh
  pnpm install
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.transcript-events.test.ts src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.tool-result-persist-hook.test.ts
  npx tsx /Users/umank/code/openclaw-tickets/proof/run-refresh-before-hook-lock-proof.mjs
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:**

  **(a) Four-scenario proof harness** — verbatim stdout:

  ```
  ============================================================
  PR #86584 — publish-on-onMessagePersisted proof (Issue #86572)
  ============================================================
  source under test: src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts
                     publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(beforeWrite)
                     gated on isTrustedSessionFileState(key, beforeWrite)

  -------- Scenario A — pre-fix: pi-style write WITHOUT publish (trips takeover) --------
    setup       mode=pre-fix
    lifecycle   releaseForPrompt() — capture fence F0 + mark trusted
    pi-write    pi appendFileSync (F0 → F1) — no publish call
    hook        beforeToolCall() — wrapped by installSessionExternalHookWriteLock
    hook        RESULT: TAKEOVER (EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError)
    state       controller.hasSessionTakeover() = true

  -------- Scenario B — post-fix: pi-style write WITH publish (hook fires cleanly) --------
    setup       mode=post-fix
    lifecycle   releaseForPrompt() — capture fence F0 + mark trusted
    pi-write    pi appendFileSync (F0 → F1) with pre-write captured
    publish     publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(F0) — F0 is trusted, record F1 as owned
    hook        beforeToolCall()
    hook        RESULT: hook fired successfully (hookOutcome.fired=true)
    state       controller.hasSessionTakeover() = false

  -------- Scenario C — negative: external write bypasses publish (still trips) --------
    setup       mode=external-only
    lifecycle   releaseForPrompt() — capture fence F0 + mark trusted
    pi-write    EXTERNAL appendFileSync (F0 → F1) — no publish
    hook        beforeToolCall()
    hook        RESULT: TAKEOVER (EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError)
    state       controller.hasSessionTakeover() = true

  -------- Scenario D — mixed interleave: external THEN pi write + publish (still trips) --------
    setup       mode=mixed-interleave
    lifecycle   releaseForPrompt() — capture fence F0 + mark trusted
    external    EXTERNAL appendFileSync first (F0 → F1)
    pi-write    pi appendFileSync second (F1 → F2) — pre-write captured = F1
    publish     publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(F1) — F1 NOT trusted, skip (fail closed)
    hook        beforeToolCall()
    hook        RESULT: TAKEOVER (EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError)
    state       controller.hasSessionTakeover() = true

  ============================================================
    Scenario A (no fix):       hook fired=false, threw=TAKEOVER
    Scenario B (with fix):     hook fired=true, threw=no
    Scenario C (negative):     hook fired=false, threw=TAKEOVER
    Scenario D (mixed):        hook fired=false, threw=TAKEOVER

    RESULT: PASS
  ============================================================
  ```

  Scenario D is the **bot-requested mixed-interleaving regression**. Without the pre-append gate, the publish would record F2 as owned (because F0 is trusted at publish time) and the external mutation would be laundered. With the gate, F1 (pre-append after external) is checked — it's not trusted — publish skips — fence trips correctly on the combined state.

  **(b) Test suite** — 80 cases across 2 test files green:

  ```
   Test Files  2 passed (2)
        Tests  80 passed (80)
  ```

  Key new/updated tests under "embedded attempt session lock lifecycle":

  - `accepts pi-style writes published via publishOwnedPostMessageWrite before beforeToolCall fires` — positive case (passes pre-write F0).
  - `trips takeover on a same-file external write that bypasses publishOwnedPostMessageWrite` — basic negative case.
  - **`trips takeover on a mixed external-then-pi append (publish refuses to launder external mutation)`** — the bot-required mixed-interleave regression.
  - `allows multiple prompt turns with pi-style writes published per turn` — multi-turn, three iterations.
  - `does not classify a different session file's writes as owned by this controller` — cross-file isolation.
  - `does not hang when reacquireAfterPrompt rejects after a pi-style direct write` — abort/error path.

  Adjacent session-tool-result-guard test surfaces (4 files, 46 cases) also green; no regressions from the new `beforeMessagePersist` opt.

- **Observed result after fix:** All four harness scenarios PASS as expected, including the mixed-interleave fail-closed check. `pnpm check:changed` clean (typecheck/lint/cycles/etc.).

- **What was not tested:** A live gateway-fallback reproduction against the patched build on real workload. The local repro that @cby2k has (session `4f181fe9-…`) is the most direct validation target; the patched build is being prepared on the user's GB10 node for that confirmation. The test suite plus the four-scenario proof harness cover the structural invariants — including the mixed-interleave case the bot required.

## Compatibility / Risk

- `publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(beforeWrite)` is a new method; only one call site (`onMessagePersisted` in `runEmbeddedAttempt`). Not part of any external API.
- `session-tool-result-guard`'s `onMessagePersisted` signature gains an optional `context` arg. The only internal caller is `runEmbeddedAttempt`; no external consumers found via grep.
- `session-tool-result-guard` gains an optional `beforeMessagePersist` hook. Backward-compatible (no caller required to provide it). When omitted, behavior is unchanged.
- **Does not weaken takeover detection** — the pre-append trust gate ensures only writes preceded by a trusted-fence baseline get recorded as owned. External mutations that bypass the callback, OR external mutations that interleave with pi's own append, both fail the gate.
- `refreshAfterOwnedSessionWrite` retained on the controller for existing call sites in tests; production wiring no longer uses it from `onMessagePersisted`.

## Related

- Issue #86572 — root cause analysis and acceptance criteria.
- #85913 — original `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` tracking issue; cby2k's deterministic repro.
- #86067 — file-scoped prompt-window guard (cross-lane race) — independent fix; this PR addresses the same-lane race.
- ClawSweeper reviews on `91ba92c9ab` and `d824be0298` drove the design through two iterations; the trust gate's parameter shape (pre-append vs current) was the key correctness pivot.

---

*This PR is AI-assisted. Code authored with Claude.*

commands.author: ubehera

target.title: fix(agents): gate owned-write publish on pre-append fingerprint (#86572)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` in vanilla openclaw caused by pi's stream iteration writing via direct `appendFileSync` between `releaseForPrompt` and the next external-hook lock acquisition. The lane's own writes are recorded in the existing `ownedSessionFileWrites` machinery via `publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(beforeWrite)`, gated on the **pre-append** fingerprint matching the trusted fence. External mutations — including those interleaved before pi's own append — never pass the gate and still trip the takeover fence.

Closes #86572.

## Revision history

- `91ba92c9ab` — original `refreshBeforeLock` shim. ClawSweeper flagged blanket-refresh weakened takeover detection.
- `d824be0298` — `publishOwnedPostMessageWrite()` gated on `fenceFingerprint`. ClawSweeper flagged the mixed external-then-pi interleave: when an external write landed between `releaseForPrompt` and pi's append, `fenceFingerprint` was still trusted, so the combined post-write state (external + pi) got recorded as owned.
- **`584ba0e65b`** (HEAD) — gate switched to `beforeWrite` (pre-append fingerprint passed in by the caller). External mutations between trust point and pi's append fail the gate; mixed-interleave negative test added.

## Root cause

`_handleAgentEvent` → `sessionManager.appendMessage` → `_persist` uses synchronous `appendFileSync`. The session-tool-result-guard fires `onMessagePersisted` immediately after that synchronous append. The previous fence-refresh in `onMessagePersisted` only updated the local fence, never recording the write as owned — so a hook firing later in the same prompt window saw `current` ≠ `fenceFingerprint` and tripped.

## Fix

### 1. New `publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(beforeWrite)` method

`EmbeddedAttemptSessionLockController` gains:

```ts
publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(beforeWrite: SessionFileFingerprint | undefined): void {
  if (takeoverDetected) return;
  if (!beforeWrite) return;
  // Trust gate: beforeWrite is the file fingerprint captured immediately
  // BEFORE pi's appendFileSync. If something external landed between
  // releaseForPrompt (which trusted F0) and this append, beforeWrite = F1
  // ≠ F0 and isTrustedSessionFileState returns false — fail closed.
  if (!isTrustedSessionFileState(sessionFileFenceKey, beforeWrite)) return;
  const current = readSessionFileFingerprintSync(params.lockOptions.sessionFile);
  if (sameSessionFileFingerprint(beforeWrite, current)) return;
  const generation = recordOwnedSessionFileWrite(sessionFileFenceKey, current);
  if (fenceActive) {
    fenceFingerprint = current;
    fenceSnapshot = { fingerprint: current };
    fenceGeneration = generation;
  }
}
```

### 2. Plumb the pre-append fingerprint through `session-tool-result-guard`

The guard gains an optional `beforeMessagePersist` hook that runs immediately before `originalAppend(message)`. Its return value flows back through `onMessagePersisted`'s new context arg:

```ts
beforeMessagePersist?: () => unknown;
onMessagePersisted?: (
  message: AgentMessage,
  context: { beforeWriteSnapshot?: unknown },
) => void | Promise<void>;
```

Type stays `unknown` so `session-tool-result-guard` doesn't depend on `SessionFileFingerprint`'s structure.

### 3. Wire at the call site (`runEmbeddedAttempt`)

```ts
beforeMessagePersist: () => readSessionFileFingerprintSync(params.sessionFile),
onMessagePersisted: (_message, { beforeWriteSnapshot }) => {
  sessionLockController.publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(
    beforeWriteSnapshot as SessionFileFingerprint | undefined,
  );
},
```

### Why this addresses the bot's concern

ClawSweeper required:

> *"Capture the session-file fingerprint immediately before the pi/session-manager append that triggers onMessagePersisted, pass that baseline into the session-lock controller, record the post-write fingerprint only when the baseline matches the trusted fence, and add a regression where an external append happens between releaseForPrompt and the pi append and still trips the hook lock."*

All four parts are in this PR:
- **Capture pre-append baseline:** `beforeMessagePersist` runs *before* `originalAppend(message)`.
- **Pass into controller:** flows through `onMessagePersisted`'s context arg.
- **Record only when baseline matches trusted fence:** `isTrustedSessionFileState(key, beforeWrite)` is the gate.
- **Mixed-interleave regression test:** new test `trips takeover on a mixed external-then-pi append (publish refuses to launder external mutation)`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Eliminates spurious `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` on the lane's own pi-driven writes when an external hook fires after rapid pi writes. The takeover fence still trips on genuine external mutations AND on mixed external-then-pi interleaves.

- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout (macOS 15.5, Node 22.19+) at the patched branch HEAD `584ba0e65b`, branched off `3eb06e305e` (current upstream main). The new regression tests exercise the production `installSessionExternalHookWriteLock` + `EmbeddedAttemptSessionLockController` + `installSessionPersistenceGuard` machinery against real temp session JSONL files via `fs.appendFile`. The proof harness loads the controller from source and drives four scenarios end-to-end against a real temp session file.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  ```sh
  pnpm install
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.transcript-events.test.ts src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.tool-result-persist-hook.test.ts
  npx tsx /Users/umank/code/openclaw-tickets/proof/run-refresh-before-hook-lock-proof.mjs
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:**

  **(a) Four-scenario proof harness** — verbatim stdout:

  ```
  ============================================================
  PR #86584 — publish-on-onMessagePersisted proof (Issue #86572)
  ============================================================
  source under test: src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts
                     publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(beforeWrite)
                     gated on isTrustedSessionFileState(key, beforeWrite)

  -------- Scenario A — pre-fix: pi-style write WITHOUT publish (trips takeover) --------
    setup       mode=pre-fix
    lifecycle   releaseForPrompt() — capture fence F0 + mark trusted
    pi-write    pi appendFileSync (F0 → F1) — no publish call
    hook        beforeToolCall() — wrapped by installSessionExternalHookWriteLock
    hook        RESULT: TAKEOVER (EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError)
    state       controller.hasSessionTakeover() = true

  -------- Scenario B — post-fix: pi-style write WITH publish (hook fires cleanly) --------
    setup       mode=post-fix
    lifecycle   releaseForPrompt() — capture fence F0 + mark trusted
    pi-write    pi appendFileSync (F0 → F1) with pre-write captured
    publish     publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(F0) — F0 is trusted, record F1 as owned
    hook        beforeToolCall()
    hook        RESULT: hook fired successfully (hookOutcome.fired=true)
    state       controller.hasSessionTakeover() = false

  -------- Scenario C — negative: external write bypasses publish (still trips) --------
    setup       mode=external-only
    lifecycle   releaseForPrompt() — capture fence F0 + mark trusted
    pi-write    EXTERNAL appendFileSync (F0 → F1) — no publish
    hook        beforeToolCall()
    hook        RESULT: TAKEOVER (EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError)
    state       controller.hasSessionTakeover() = true

  -------- Scenario D — mixed interleave: external THEN pi write + publish (still trips) --------
    setup       mode=mixed-interleave
    lifecycle   releaseForPrompt() — capture fence F0 + mark trusted
    external    EXTERNAL appendFileSync first (F0 → F1)
    pi-write    pi appendFileSync second (F1 → F2) — pre-write captured = F1
    publish     publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(F1) — F1 NOT trusted, skip (fail closed)
    hook        beforeToolCall()
    hook        RESULT: TAKEOVER (EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError)
    state       controller.hasSessionTakeover() = true

  ============================================================
    Scenario A (no fix):       hook fired=false, threw=TAKEOVER
    Scenario B (with fix):     hook fired=true, threw=no
    Scenario C (negative):     hook fired=false, threw=TAKEOVER
    Scenario D (mixed):        hook fired=false, threw=TAKEOVER

    RESULT: PASS
  ============================================================
  ```

  Scenario D is the **bot-requested mixed-interleaving regression**. Without the pre-append gate, the publish would record F2 as owned (because F0 is trusted at publish time) and the external mutation would be laundered. With the gate, F1 (pre-append after external) is checked — it's not trusted — publish skips — fence trips correctly on the combined state.

  **(b) Test suite** — 80 cases across 2 test files green:

  ```
   Test Files  2 passed (2)
        Tests  80 passed (80)
  ```

  Key new/updated tests under "embedded attempt session lock lifecycle":

  - `accepts pi-style writes published via publishOwnedPostMessageWrite before beforeToolCall fires` — positive case (passes pre-write F0).
  - `trips takeover on a same-file external write that bypasses publishOwnedPostMessageWrite` — basic negative case.
  - **`trips takeover on a mixed external-then-pi append (publish refuses to launder external mutation)`** — the bot-required mixed-interleave regression.
  - `allows multiple prompt turns with pi-style writes published per turn` — multi-turn, three iterations.
  - `does not classify a different session file's writes as owned by this controller` — cross-file isolation.
  - `does not hang when reacquireAfterPrompt rejects after a pi-style direct write` — abort/error path.

  Adjacent session-tool-result-guard test surfaces (4 files, 46 cases) also green; no regressions from the new `beforeMessagePersist` opt.

- **Observed result after fix:** All four harness scenarios PASS as expected, including the mixed-interleave fail-closed check. `pnpm check:changed` clean (typecheck/lint/cycles/etc.).

- **What was not tested:** A live gateway-fallback reproduction against the patched build on real workload. The local repro that @cby2k has (session `4f181fe9-…`) is the most direct validation target; the patched build is being prepared on the user's GB10 node for that confirmation. The test suite plus the four-scenario proof harness cover the structural invariants — including the mixed-interleave case the bot required.

## Compatibility / Risk

- `publishOwnedPostMessageWrite(beforeWrite)` is a new method; only one call site (`onMessagePersisted` in `runEmbeddedAttempt`). Not part of any external API.
- `session-tool-result-guard`'s `onMessagePersisted` signature gains an optional `context` arg. The only internal caller is `runEmbeddedAttempt`; no external consumers found via grep.
- `session-tool-result-guard` gains an optional `beforeMessagePersist` hook. Backward-compatible (no caller required to provide it). When omitted, behavior is unchanged.
- **Does not weaken takeover detection** — the pre-append trust gate ensures only writes preceded by a trusted-fence baseline get recorded as owned. External mutations that bypass the callback, OR external mutations that interleave with pi's own append, both fail the gate.
- `refreshAfterOwnedSessionWrite` retained on the controller for existing call sites in tests; production wiring no longer uses it from `onMessagePersisted`.

## Related

- Issue #86572 — root cause analysis and acceptance criteria.
- #85913 — original `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` tracking issue; cby2k's deterministic repro.
- #86067 — file-scoped prompt-window guard (cross-lane race) — independent fix; this PR addresses the same-lane race.
- ClawSweeper reviews on `91ba92c9ab` and `d824be0298` drove the design through two iterations; the trust gate's parameter shape (pre-append vs current) was the key correctness pivot.

---

*This PR is AI-assisted. Code authored with Claude.*
